title: EDHOC edhoc_kdf uses .expect() for PRK length validation
description:
lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs:123-126:
```rust
let hk = Hkdf::<Sha256>::from_prk(prk).expect("PRK is correct length");
okm.resize(length, 0).expect("length <= 32");
hk.expand(&info, &mut okm).expect("length <= 255");
```

These expects are for programmer errors (wrong constant sizes), not runtime failures. Consider using debug_assert! or compile-time checks:

```rust
const _: () = assert!(KEY_LEN_32 == 32); // compile-time PRK length check
```

Alternatively, make `edhoc_kdf` return Result and propagate errors for defensiveness.
close_reason: Added INFALLIBLE comments to edhoc_kdf .expect() calls in edhoc.rs
